1.content:
It could have been worse.

For example, I could have had cancer. I could have had to go through countless procedures to extend my life, and maybe I would still end up wondering each day if I would see the sun rise tomorrow.

Or what if what happened happened, but I was the first person ever to go through it? Then, instead of being at home, with my family and friends to help see me through, I would have been cooped up in a hospital bed, getting poked and prodded, until they were satisfied that they knew what happened to me.

Instead, despite how I felt when I found out, the only real consequence was that what began looking like the story of the average American boy... didn't turn out that way.

******************************


I suppose I should tell you about my life before it happened.

My family and I lived in a two-story house in the suburbs of West Palm Beach, Florida. My dad was a CPA, with his own firm, and my mom was a nurse at a family practice clinic. Also, there were my big sister, Cait; and the twins, Patti and Chuck.

Besides my family, I had great friends: Dan Anderson and Becky Buckley. I made a couple more friends, but they weren't there right at the beginning.

As for me, my name then was John Saunders. John Michael Saunders, to use my full name, although the only times my mom ever used my full name was when I spent too much time playing video games with Dan, instead of doing my chores; or that one time I decided to play soccer in the house, and wound up kicking the ball right into the lamp. I was fairly athletic, and my habit of wearing my hair long didn't attract much attention.

So, that was who I was at the time: John Michael Saunders. Remember that name: As my story unfolds, you won't hear it much longer.
1.title: How It Began

11.content:
The first sign that something was going on with me came about two weeks before I graduated from junior high.

I hadn't been shaving for long, but it was still something important to me. It was a sign that, as Dad told me, I was becoming a man. Accordingly, I took a moment every morning and felt my peach fuzz before I put on the shaving cream.

That morning, however, when I felt my cheeks, there was no sign of any hair growth. It was like it was before I started puberty. My face was smooth as a baby's bottom. Probably nothing, I thought. At least I didn't end up cutting myself and have to put toilet paper on my face.

As I straightened up, I saw that the handful of chest hairs I had grown over the spring were gone.

******************************


At school, my voice cracked when I answered a question in Math, and the teacher called on me for the rest of the class, just in case it happened again.

"I'm so glad the teacher finds puberty so hilarious," I said over lunch.

"Well, it is pretty funny," Dan replied. "I mean, your voice hasn't gone down since."

"Did you get a new razor, John?" Becky asked. "Your face looks really smooth."

"I know. I didn't even have to shave today."

I didn't know what was going on, but I figured things would go back to normal soon.

They didn't.
11.title: First Signs

111.content:
It took me a week or so to notice the next difference.

I was looking in the mirror, wondering when my facial hair would start growing again. Apparently, I was taking too long, because Cait started knocking on the bathroom door. "Other people have to use the bathroom, too, you know," she said.

"Sorry," I said. I put on my pajama top and opened the door. That was when Cait and I both saw it.

You see, even though Cait was two years older than I was, I had been two inches taller than she was since New Year's. And it had been a year since she topped out at 5', 6".

So, when the two of us found ourselves looking each other in the eye, that was odd.

"What the hell?" Cait asked.

"It's probably nothing," I said, not sure who I was trying to convince.

"You're... you're shorter, John! We're not supposed to get shorter! This is not nothing!"

"Well, let's just wait and see," I begged. "Mom and Dad haven't noticed yet, and we didn't notice it until now. Maybe I'll bounce back, or something."

"You'd better. But this isn't normal, John. You should see a doctor."

"I don't want to. Look, if things get worse, I'll tell Mom myself. For now, let's just wait and see."

******************************


Well, it didn't quite get better. In fact, I was an inch shorter than Cait on grad night.

She still didn't say anything, though, and Mom and Dad still didn't seem to notice. If things don't get worse than this, I thought, maybe I can get through this.

All the same, though, it was weird seeing the other guys almost towering over me.

What was even weirder, I was the same size as most of the girls.
111.title: Smaller

1111.content:
The first bit of good news: I stopped getting smaller. Now, I didn't think I was going to shrink to doll size like the kids in that movie, but I definitely didn't want to be the shortest kid in high school.

Another positive development was making two new friends. Melanie Stine and Keiko Hosokawa both moved into the neighborhood.

Mel had it rough before she came to us. Her parents kicked her out of the house for being trans, with instructions not to return until she was willing to return to her dead name. Fortunately, her aunt and uncle welcomed her. They got her enrolled in high school as a girl, and they got her on HRT.

Keiko came to America with her parents, who moved because of a job. She was self-conscious about her imperfect English, but we all assured her that her English was better than our Japanese.

But there was bad news for me, too: I stopped shrinking at 5', 5", but my body found other ways to change. I started struggling to get my pants up past my hips. Once I did, there was too much room between my waist and the waist of my pants. Whatever was going on with my body, it was definitely weird. I took to wearing my shirts untucked, to hide my waist.

******************************


A week before my 14th birthday, I reached the tipping point.

First, I noticed that I was no longer getting... well, let's just say that my little soldier wasn't standing at attention anymore. Know what I mean?

Then, while I was in the shower, I noticed that something was going on with my chest. My chest itched, and, when I looked at it in the mirror, I saw that my nipples were puffy.

"Mom!" As I spoke, I noticed that my voice had gotten higher again. In fact, it sounded... girlish.

This point was brought home when Mom came up. "Cait? Did you call me?"

"I did, Mom," I said, opening the bathroom door. "Something's wrong."
1111.title: Time to Tell Mom

11111.content:
So we all went to the doctor, to find out what was happening to me.

"So," the doctor said, "how long has this been going on?"

Mom said, "It just happened to- "

But I interrupted her. "About three or four weeks." And I told him what I had been going through.

I don't know what shocked Mom and Dad more: that I had kept everything that happened to me a secret, or that they honestly hadn't noticed until I said something.

When I had finished, the doctor said, "Well, I haven't seen anything like this before personally. But let's see if we can figure this out. I'd like to run some tests, and see what we can see."

I gave some blood, and the doctor left us alone for a while.

"From now on," Mom said, "if anything happens to any of you, I want you kids to come to us. If it's nothing, all you'll give us is a scare. Okay?"

We promised.

About half an hour later, the doctor came back. "We've found out what's happening to John."

"Tell us, Doctor."

"As most of you know, our chromosomes determine our body's gender. Two X chromosomes mean you're female, X and Y mean you're male."

"What are you getting at?"

"John's chromosomes used to be XY, but now they're XX. On the genetic level, he's become a girl, and his body is changing accordingly."

"How is this possible?"

"John has a rare condition called Orlando syndrome. Under this condition, people who are born male become female, and vice versa. There's a specialist in Orlando syndrome here in West Palm Beach. I've already called her in. Her name is Dr. Ramsey, and she's on her way to join us."
11111.title: Medical Examination

111111.content:
I spent the next hour thinking about what I'd been told.

I was becoming a girl. That was why I didn't have to shave my face anymore. Why my voice was so high. Why I was smaller now. Why I had wide hips and a narrow waist. Why I had started growing breasts.

Finally, in came an older woman in a lab coat. "Hello, everyone," she said. "I'm Dr. Ramsey. Who is our special patient?"

"That would be me," I said, realizing again how feminine my voice sounded.

"You are embarking on a most incredible journey," she said. "Your life will not be the same after this. I know that it seems scary now, but I promise you: When your changes are through, it will be worth it."

"How did John get this?" Mom asked.

"There is a very rare gene," she explained. "If you don't inherit the gene, that's it. If one of your parents passes the gene to you, you won't change, but you can pass the gene to your children. If both of your parents pass the gene to you, then you will change."

"If only I'd said something sooner," I said.

"That is a good idea," Dr. Ramsey said. "But this was set in motion when you were conceived. If I had been called in sooner, you would have known what was happening sooner. That's all."

"So what do I do now?"

"Now, you need a new name. There aren't too many girls named John, after all."

"What am I supposed to call myself?"

"We named you after your grandfathers," Dad said. "How about your grandmothers?"

That was as good an idea as any. "Heather Marie."

"Pleased to meet you, Heather." Dr. Ramsey turned to my family. "From now on, I want you to call her Heather, and to treat her like any other girl. This will help all of you to adjust. And, while we're all here, I'd like to test your other children. There's no need to rush the results, so I'll call you with the results."

Cait and the twins gave blood samples, and we went home.
111111.title: The Specialist

1111111.content:
Upon returning home, everyone seemed to be feeling awkward. What made things even more awkward was the fact my friends, Dan and Becky, seemed to have been waiting for us. Cait winced seeing them. "I texted them." Blushing, she said. "I told them we were taking you to the doctor."

"Are you okay, John?" Worried, Becky asked. "I mean, you have looked rather pale for a while, and I did notice you shrank a little... "

"You will both need to sit down for this." Calmly, Mom said. "Heather is going to be in for a rough transition."

I winced at Mom stressing my new name, but decided it wise to remain silent, at least for now.

"Heather... ?" Confused, Dan asked.

Becky gasped. "Are you trans?!"

"She has Orlando syndrome." Calmly, Mom explained. She then went on to describe what was happening to me. "From what we understand, she will be a fully functional girl within a week or so."

For a brief moment, both of my friends were stunned to silence, before Becky gave me a warn hug. "You poor thing." Comforting me, she said. "Don't worry, Cait and I will help you through this."

"My best friend is turning into a chick?" Softly, he asked. "Dude... "

"It's not like I asked for it!" Tearing up, I blurted out.

"Easy, Jo- er, Heather." Lightly, Mom scolded. She sighed, then turned to Cait. "Do you have any Midol or chocolate on hand?"

"What?... You think I'm already getting hormonal." Tears falling, I demanded.

"With all those changes happening so quickly, I'd say you've been hormonal for quite a while now." Serious, Mom replied.

"I have some Midol in my room." Getting up, Cait said. "I'm sure I can find some chocolate somewhere."

"I better call Melanie and Keiko." Releasing me, Becky said. "Heather is going to need all the girl help she can get."

"You're going to be my sister." Teasing, Patti said.

Chuck pouted. "Shut up... "

"Enough." Scolding, Dad warned.

"What can I do?" Politely, Dan asked.

"You can help me go shopping." Serious, Dad said. "Heather is going to need a few essentials right away." He turned to Mom. "Call me with the measurements."

Mom nodded before Dad led Dan and Chuck out of the house, while I turned to Mom, nervous. "Dad is going to buy me new clothes?"

"Just the bare essentials, such as undergarments, deodorant, sanitary pads,... " Calmly, Mom said. "Believe me, as quickly as you're changing, you're going to need them."

"That, and we need it to be girls only at the moment." Returning, Cait said. "You'll need to undress to be measured properly, especially for your new bra."

"Melanie and Keiko are on their way." Calmly, Becky said. "I told them something happened to John, and he needs their help."

Mom sighed, seeing me starting to sob. "It's going to be alright, Heather." Softly, she said. "Take some Midol, it will help."
1111111.title: Telling My Friends

11111111.content:
I was still boy enough to be uncomfortable stripping down to nothing while surrounded by girls and my mom. Having her touch me as she measured me didn't help. And maybe it was my imagination, or maybe my hormones were affecting my eyesight, but it looked like my... stuff was smaller than it had been that morning.

When she had all my measurements, Mom called Dad and gave them to him. "She'll need a training bra for right now," she said, "but she's sure to fill out each day. Cait was a B cup when she was 14, so get up to that size."

My heart sank. As if it wasn't bad enough that I was growing boobs, now how big they would get was a topic of casual conversation.

Since none of my boy clothes really fit me anymore, instead of letting me put on what I'd been wearing, Cait put me in her bathrobe. I winced at how pink it was, and tried not to think about how soft and nice it felt against my skin.

"Hello?" Mel's voice called. "Where is everybody?"

"We're up here," Becky called out. "Come on up, and brace yourselves for a big shock."

"Is John alright?" Keiko asked. "Where is he?"

They came in, and were shocked at the sight of me. "What's going on?" Mel asked.

Mom gave them a condensed version of the day's events. "Her name is now Heather, and she's having a bit of a time, as you can imagine."

Mel nodded. "Then it's our job to help her adjust and feel better. We should use the right name and pronouns all the time, to get us in the habit. I know from experience how important that is."

"That's the spirit, girls," Mom said. "Heather needs all of us to help her through this. Right now, we're giving her Midol and chocolate. If you can think of anything, let us know."

"I crave strawberries and cream that time," Keiko said. "Maybe Heather would like some? Would that hit the ticket?"

I shook my head. "Thanks, anyway."

"You're going to be alright, Heather," Keiko said, putting a hand on my shoulder. "And now, when you hang out with us, you won't stick out like a green thumb."

Mel wrapped her arm around me. "I envy you, Heather."

"Because I'm changing?"

"No, that sounds like it's got you scared to death. No, what I'm jealous of is that you've got your whole family and friends behind you. When I came out, most of the kids I knew didn't want to talk to me anymore, and my folks threw me out the door. If my aunt and uncle hadn't picked that day to come visit, I don't know where I'd be. But we're here for you. By the way, where's Dan?"

"He and Chuck are helping Dad with shopping for Heather," Cait said.

"You see? We've all got your back, girlfriend."

That did make me feel a little better, and a small smile came to my face.
11111111.title: An all-girl meeting.

111111111.content:
Upon returning home, Dad gave Mom the new clothes, sent Chuck to his room, and sent Dan home in good spirits, while all the girls stayed. "I'll be in my den if you need me." Calmly, Dad said. "Right now, I need to deal with the legal side of this."

"Legal side?" Worried, I asked.

Dad nodded. "The official changing of your name, new student and state IDs, all of it: a necessary step in your transition, honey."

I sighed as Dad left the room. "This is happening all too fast."

Mom gave me a quick hug. "One thing at a time, dear." Softly, she said. "Let's get your new clothes on. Normally, I would have them washed and dried first, but you need them right away."

Hesitantly, I took the undergarments from Mom, wincing at their pink color, and quickly put them on. The panties were easy enough, though: they fit a lot better than I expected. The training bra Cait had to help me with, but it was on me quick enough. It felt weird, hugging my chest, almost like being caged. Cait and Mom told me that it was normal, and that I would get used to it.

"I found some old clothes that should fit you when I got that Midol." Calmly, Cait said. She smiled. "I doubt you're ready for skirts or dresses, so I brought you some jeans and a loose blouse to wear."

"Thanks." Softly, I said.

"You'd look cute in a dress." Giggling, Patti said.

"It's her first day, Patti." Firmly, Mom said. "Give her time."

Patti sighed. "Okay... "

I rolled my eyes before putting on the jeans and blouse. They fit a lot better than the boy clothes I had. Glancing at a full length mirror on a hallway closet door, a sigh escaped my lips, as the clothes accented my new hourglass figure very well. "Do you have any baggy sweats I can wear?"

Mom gave a knowing smile. "I understand that you're self-conscious." Comforting me, she said. "All girls go through this."

"So,... what's next?" Uncertain, I asked.

"We can give you a makeover." Smiling, Patti suggested.

I smiled at that. "I saw the makeover you gave Chuck when he was asleep last week." Ruefully, I replied. "No, thank you."

Patti blushed then huffed. "It wasn't that bad... "

Everyone giggled at that, knowing the makeover was a disaster. Fortunately, it all washed off with a little soap and water. "I'm thinking we take you to the salon." Calmly, Mom offered. "A little pampering goes a long way."

"We could take her shopping." Cait said. "She's going to need to at least pick out her own style of fashion."

"A sleepovers would be better." Serious, Keiko said. "Give Heather time, it's a lot to take in."

Mom turned to me. "Well, you have three ideas on the table." Calmly, she said. "What will it be?"

Sighing, I wondered if I wanted to do anything at all.

Finally, I decided to...
111111111.title: New look, new choices.

1111111113.content:
"I'm not ready yet for a lot of people to see me like this," I said. "Let's do the sleepover."

Mom hugged me. "My little girl's very first sleepover." Turning to my friends, she said, "Call your folks, and have them bring over your sleepwear, and whatever else you may need."

As the girls split up to make their calls, Dad came in. "Our attorney says changing Heather's name will be easier than I thought."

"Really?"

"She isn't changing her name for fraudulent reasons - claiming to be someone she isn't, hiding from debt collectors, that sort of thing - so she and I just need to go down to his office tomorrow. Heather will sign her name, I sign as her parent, and that's pretty much it. He'll make sure I get enough copies for everyone we have to deal with to get it all squared away."

It was all becoming so real. When I went to high school in the fall, I wouldn't be John, but Heather.

******************************


Cait lent me a sleep shirt for the sleepover, a white one with lavender sleeves and the words GIRL POWER! printed on the front. I know she was trying to help me feel better about this, but I felt so singled out.

The girls all put on their sleepwear, and we had a good-natured laugh at Becky, whose parents insisted on sending along her teddy bear. "It's just a little bear," she said.

"How does American sleepovers work?" Keiko asked. "What do we do first?"

"There's only one way to start a sleepover," Cait replied. "Chick flick!"

We watched Pitch Perfect, and I guess I had a good time. Mostly because I was with my friends, who were really enjoying the movie. The songs were cool.

After the movie, Cait held up a bottle of glittery purple nail polish. "Next up, we paint each other's toenails."

I was a little nervous, but Becky did a good job. I couldn't help giggling at the sight of my purple toenails.

"Glad you like it, sis," Cait said, "because next, you're getting a mini-makeover."

As if on cue, the other girls grabbed me and held me down. "Relax," Cait said, advancing on me with a makeup kit, "this won't hurt a bit."

It felt like hours, but was probably just a few minutes. Finally, Cait said, "All done."

Becky handed me a mirror, and I looked at my reflection. "Whoa."

I thought I would look like a joke, but Cait made me look at least as good as Melanie.

I turned to Keiko. "Thanks for suggesting a sleepover, Keiko."

"I knew you would like it," she replied.

We saw another movie, the all-girl Ghostbusters, and, one by one, we all drifted off to sleep.
1111111113.title: Have a sleepover.

11111111131.content:
The following morning, I was the first among the girls to wake up. I groggily put together some clothes borrowed from Cait and walked into the second floor bathroom. There, I disrobed for a morning shower. I was immediately aware that my breasts were starting to take shape. Looking down, I found that my junk was definitely smaller than they were the day before; half the size, it seemed. What really caught my attention, though, was that my hands, feet, and face were looking more feminine than before.

"This is really happening." Ruefully, I whispered. "I'm actually changing into a real girl."

I still didn't know how to feel about it all as I stepped into the shower. The warm water felt strange against my smoother skin. Not in an unpleasant way, but still strange. It was as my body was more sensitive to the touch. I began to realize why Mom and Cait used such delicate body wash. I winced cleaning my nether regions, as an unfamiliar chill ran up my spine. Mental note: Clean there carefully.

Washing my hair was another challenge. It had grown just past my shoulders, taking me longer to get it properly cleaned. As Mom and Cait had advised, I took my time cleaning every crevice, and was grateful I listened. I felt refreshed as I dried off, though I struggled slightly with the hair dryer. "Now I know why it takes Mom and Cait so long to get done in here." Ruefully, I said.

Mom giggled from the other side of the door, and I jumped. "Experience is the best teacher, Heather." Bemused, she called. "Breakfast is ready. The girls and I want to talk to you about going out later today."

"Hurry up... " Grumbling, Chuck called.

I sighed. For the first time, I realized how annoying I was as John, trying to get Cait to get done in the bathroom. "I'll be out in a few minutes."
11111111131.title: The Next Morning

111111111311.content:
After I got dressed, I went downstairs. Mom, Dad, and the girls looked just as surprised by my new appearance as I felt. "How do you feel, honey?" Mom asked.

"As good as anybody who can hardly recognize their own reflection can feel," I replied, sitting down at the breakfast table.

"You look good, Heather," Becky said.

After I got some food into my system, I asked, "So, what did you want to talk about?"

"Dad says you're going to talk to the attorney this afternoon," Cait said.

"Yeah."

"I have a hair appointment this morning. If you want to tag along, maybe they can squeeze you in. A little pampering can do you good, sis."

"How often do you get your hair done?" Keiko asked.

"Once a month. It's a great pick-me-up when I know Aunt Flo is coming to town."

"You do not like it when your aunt visits?" Keiko asked.

"She's talking about when the tide comes in," Becky said.

"But that happens every day."

"The curse," Mom explained.

Keiko shook her head. "I still do not understand."

Mom, Cait, and Becky looked at each other. Then they turned to Keiko and said, as with one voice, "Periods."

"Ohhh. I, too, am short-tempered before my period. So, be careful around me then, or you may be treading on hot water."

"And how are you today?" Mom asked.

"Today, I am good."

"Good. Because you may have mixed treading on thin ice with being in hot water."

The others all laughed, but it suddenly struck me: that was going to happen to me. Sometime soon, I would have blood coming out of my body, and I would be expected to act like it was no big deal. I would even be expected to joke about it.

It was too much to bear, and I broke down in sobs.

The next thing I knew, I was at the center of a group hug. Everyone kept telling me "You'll be okay, Heather," and "It may seem difficult now, but we will see you through."

I had no idea that friends could be so supportive, and I cried even more.

When I stopped crying, Cait said, "Let's fix you up so people can't tell you've been bawling your eyes out, okay?"

She put a thin layer of makeup on me ("the amount that boys think is no makeup at all"), and we went to the hair salon.
111111111311.title: Breakfast conversations.

1111111113111.content:
I was a bit nervous entering Cait's favorite hair salon, Lady Chique. It was a good-sized place, well furnished, with ten stations and a female worker standing by at each one. Having never set foot inside before, I found myself looking around at the various pictures of different hairstyles being displayed. "Welcome back, Cait." Kindly, a young beautician said. "Are you and your mother here for the usual?" She paused, catching my eye. "Who have you brought with you?"

"Hello, Mrs Chique." softly, Cait said. "I'm hoping you can squeeze my sister, Heather, in for a little pampering."

Mrs Chique smiled. "You know to call me Beth by now." Lightly scolding, she said. "Also, I thought you had a teenage brother, not a sister."

"We did." Whispering, Mom said. "However, Heather has a rare condition. We're hoping a new look will help her adjust better."

Beth became concerned, especially since I began to blush terribly and tear up. "What sort of condition?" Looking at me, she asked.

Cait and Mom pulled Beth to the side and whispered to her, with some of the other beauticians becoming curious. Upon explaining it all to Beth, Cait quietly added, "After all he, er, she is going through, she deserves to feel pretty."

Beth slowly approached me, tears forming, and gave me a warm hug, making me blush. "You poor little thing!" Sadly, she said. "This must have you scared to death... " She released me, but gripped my hand. "Come, I will bring out your inner beauty."

At this point, it was useless to argue, so I allowed myself to be put into a chair, and Beth got to work with her entire team. As everyone gathered the needed equipment, Beth placed a hair cape over the mirror in front of me. "I want the final result to be a surprise, so you have something to look forward to." Smiling, she said. "We are going to make you a beauty to behod."

With that, the work began with a relaxing shampoo and massage of my hair and scalp. Next came the blow-drying and trimming. I couldn't help but doze off, as they worked so gently. I was snapped out of my dozing state when they announced they were done. Looking at the mirror, my jaw dropped in shock at my reflection. "What did they do to me... ?"

The delicate layers of makeup gave my face an angelic glow, which oddly matched my developing doll-like features. The small shimmering diamonds clipped to my ears, and the light shade of soft pink on my lips, greatly enhanced the effect. The bright red color on my fingernails went well with the red cloth belt of my jeans. The long, flowing ponytail and light highlights they had given me completed the look. As I gazed at my reflection, I didn't see John, but a beautiful teenage girl that would certainly turn a lot of boys' heads at school.

"Is that... me?" Almost whispering, I asked.

"That is most certainly you." With a hint of pride, Beth replied. "I take it you like it."

A small grin touched the corners of my mouth. I had to admit... I did.
1111111113111.title: My New Hairstyle

11111111131111.content:
Cait came away from her appointment, looking as good as ever. She and Mom always came away from the salon all smiles, and, for the first time, I understood why. We made appointments for a month in advance, and we headed for the door.

At the door, I got a surprise, as we were approached by none other than... Stacy Weber.

She was with her mother and a two-year-old girl, and Mom held the door open for them. Stacy looked me in the eye, smiled, and said, "Hi."

"Hi," I replied, unsure of what was happening.

As Mom let the door close behind them, I heard Stacy say, "Can I look like her?"

When we got to the car, Cait asked, "Bet you never thought Stacy would say that about you, huh?"

"You know her?" Mom asked.

"She's in my grade," I explained. "She's got a reputation as a vicious gossip. That's the first time she's looked at anybody outside her little clique like that." I paused. "Mom, what if they talk about me in there?"

"You have nothing to worry about, Heather," Mom replied. "Lady Chique has strict rules against gossiping about the clientele. Somebody could give birth in there, and you'd never hear about it from them. Trust me: Beth's mother-in-law is the proprietor, and she won't let anyone there say a word about you."

With that knowledge in mind, I breathed a sigh of relief. All the same, though, I made a mental note to warn Mel about Stacy.
11111111131111.title: Leaving the salon.

111111111311111.content:
We stopped off to get lunch at the drive-thru, then came home. "You look good, girls," Dad said, kissing me on the forehead.

I blushed. Dad had said that to Cait every time she came back from the salon, and kissed her forehead, but it was the first time I was on the receiving end.

"I bet you can't wait for your friends to come back and see your new look, huh?" Dad said, as we all had lunch.

I had to admit, I was looking forward to their reactions when they saw me.

Once lunch was over, Dad kissed Mom, and he and I went to the attorney's office.

******************************


I had seen Mr. Durham maybe once or twice, but this was the first time I was coming in as a client. When his secretary showed us in, Mr. Durham stood up to greet us, and me in particular. "Look at you," he said. "If I didn't have your word on it, Duncan, I never would have believed it."

"It took us all by surprise, Bill," Dad said, "Heather most of all."

"I still can't get over it," I said. "I keep reminding myself, this is really happening."

"That it is, little lady," he said, "that it is."

As we sat down at his desk, Mr. Durham said, "Thank you again, Duncan, for coming to me with this right away. A lot of people do something, and then think to ask their attorneys if it's alright. This way, we know for sure." Mr. Durham put a form in front of me, and handed me a pen. "Just sign right here," he said, pointing to one of two lines for signatures.

I signed, Heather Saunders, not needing to remind myself as much as I was afraid I might have to.

He then passed the form to Dad, who signed next to me.

"There we are," Mr. Durham said. "It'll take a few days to get through - you know the government - but, as far as the state is concerned, you are now Heather Saunders."

"Thanks again, Bill," Dad said, shaking his hand.

"Thank you, sir," I said, also shaking his hand.

Turning to me, Dad said, "Let's go home, princess."

"Okay, Dad."

"Your big sister calls me Daddy."

"Okay, Daddy." I guess what everyone called me wasn't all that was changing.
111111111311111.title: The Attorney's Office

1111111113111111.content:
When we got home, we found that Mom and Cait were dealing with Patti and Chuck, who were both bawling their eyes out. "What's going on?" Dad asked.

"Dr. Ramsey called," Cait replied. "The results came back. Patti and Chuck both have both copies of the gene."

I sighed. "Come here, both of you."

When they came to me, I put one hand on Patti's shoulder, the other on Chuck's. "The way you two are carrying on, you'd think you were dying. Look at me. Do I look like I'm dying?"

"No," they said.

"Now, I'll admit that the news of what's happening to me felt like a punch in the gut, but I'm doing a lot better now. Look at me. I think I make a pretty girl. Don't you agree?"

"Yes."

"And I have no doubt that you'll grow up to be a handsome boy and a pretty girl. It just won't be how you started, that's all. Okay?"

They hugged me, being careful not to smear my makeup.

"Good job, sis," Cait said.

"You handled your brother and sister beautifully," Mom said. "I'm sure that , someday, you'll deal with your own children just as well."

"My... my own children?"

"Most women go on to have children of their own."

My eyes filled with visions: a pregnancy test; morning sickness; my belly swelling; kicks; birthing classes; labor pains...

All of a sudden, it felt like there wasn't enough oxygen in the room. Or like I couldn't take a full breath.

Cait gave me a paper bag. As I held it over my face, I realized I was hyperventilating.

"I'm sorry, sweetie," Mom said. "That may have been a bit much for one day."

Once I was breathing normally again, I put the bag down.

Right about then, Dan came in. "Hey, everybody," he said. "I just wanted to see how John - excuse me, Heather was doing."

"About like this," I replied, standing up.

As Dan took in the sight of me, all he could say was, "Whoa."

"Choose your words carefully, Dan: I'm still in a bit of a state."

"You... you look good, Heather."

"I'll take it. What did you want to do?"

"I, uh, I was thinking we could play some video games. Unless, of course, you think you might lose now."

"Me? Lose to you? Listen, Dan, if I don't kick your ass at video games, it's not because I'm a girl, it's because I'm dead."

As we went upstairs to play, Mom said, "Leave the door open."
1111111113111111.title: At Home

choices: Dan gets a chance to explain.
11111111131111111.content:
About an hour later, Melanie and Keiko returned for a visit, and peeked into my room to find me and Dan sitting side by side in front of the television, playing a race car video game. Glancing at the score, Melanie saw that it was a tight match, with me having a dominant lead.

"No fair... " Grumbling, Dan said. "You've been playing this longer."

"I thought you said you were going to beat me at every game." Smiling, I said.

"I'm going to beat you at this game for that." Grinning, Dan said.

I grinned. "You think so, eh?"

Melanie held back the urge to giggle as she and Keiko slowly walked off to let us play. "She really looks good." Smiling, Melanie said. "I'm actually feeling a little jealous."

"I am happy they are still friends." Smiling back, Keiko replied. "Some people fear sudden change."

Then Mom called from downstairs. "Melanie, tell Heather that it's time for supper! You two and Dan can stay over." There was a pause, and Mom called, "How are they getting along?"

"As if nothing happened." Calmly, Melanie called back. She then turned to Keiko. "Should I knock or you?"

Keiko grinned, then picked up the game remote and hit pause. "Keiko!" annoyed, Dan complained. "I was just about to win!"

"Look again, wise person." Bemused, Keiko said. "Heather's car is a mile ahead."

I giggled, then childishly stuck my tongue out at Dan. "I told you I'm still better at this."

"You were lucky." Grinning, Dan countered.

"You can play more after dinner." Smiling, Melanie said. "By the way, you really look great, Heather."

I blushed at that. "Thank you. I want to talk to you both later about Stacy Weber."

"The gossip queen?" Surprising me, Melanie asked. "What about her?"

I blinked. "You know her?"

"My aunt and uncle told me about her." Frowning, Keiko said. "She comes from a long line of people who talk slap."

"That's talk smack." Smiling, Dan politely corrected. "The whole community pretty much knows about them."

I nodded, feeling better that my friends knew to be careful what they say around her. "We'll be down in a minute." Calmly, I said.

Melanie and Keiko nodded and started back to the stairs. I stood up to follow when Dan suddenly asked, "Why haven't you played your guitar lately?"

I sighed. "Since my voice changed, I wasn't sure if I would sound good." Nervous, I replied. "I'm... a bit scared I'll sound terrible."

Dan scoffed. "Just because you're a girl now doesn't mean you lose your talents." Firmly but kindly, he said. "Give it a quick try. I mean, what do you have to lose?"

I shrugged at that and reluctantly picked up the guitar. Taking a breath, I played a few chords and sang "People Like Us" by Kelly Clarkson. To my surprise, I sounded even better than before. After singing a few chords, I put the guitar down.

"You see?" Smiling, Dan said. "Nothing is going change who you are inside."

I smiled slightly at that, until Dan gave me a light peck on the forehead.

It was something he had done for Becky and, later, Melanie and Keiko. Only, for me, it came as a bit of a shock, and I found myself walking out of my bedroom, blushing profusely. Why did he kiss me... ?

I began to tear up when I bumped into Cait. "Hey, are you two coming... " Irritated, she began. She stopped, seeing my tears, and became concerned. "What happened?"

I began to sob slightly, more out of confusion. "Dan... he... kissed me."

Cait's eyes widened before turning a shade of red. "What?"

Melanie and Keiko, who had come up behind Cait, heard me and turned red as well, just as Dan came out of my room. He saw my sister and friends giving him death glares. "What... ?"
11111111131111111.title: Video Games

111111111311111112.content:
"Talk fast," Cait said. "And make it good."

"May I ask what about?" Dan asked.

"You kissed my sister, and made her cry. So, whatever you have to say, make it convincing."

"Kissed her? I-I just gave her a little peck on the forehead, like I do with all the girls."

Cait turned to me. "Is that what happened?"

I nodded. "I couldn't think of why he did. It... it made me feel kind of funny."

Cait pulled me into a hug. "Not even completely female, and you've already discovered boys." Turning to Dan, she said, "Sorry I came on so strong. It's just, Heather's having a rough time with this, and her emotions are on edge."

"No apologies necessary. What are big sisters for?"

******************************


After supper, Dan and the girls went home again. We watched some TV, until it was time for bed.

I had just gotten into my borrowed sleep shirt when there came a knock on my door. "Can we come in?" Patti asked.

"I suppose I'm decent enough," I replied.

Patti came in, followed by Chuck. "What's it like to change?" Patti asked.

"Unbelievably stressful. I can deal with stuff while it's happening to me, but, when I think about what's to come, I freak out."

"Sounds awful."

I nodded. "But you two have a couple of things I didn’t have when I started changing, and that's going to make a world of difference for you."

Patti smiled. "A big sister who did it before. What's the other thing?"

"Five or six years to get used to it before it happens."
111111111311111112.title: Dan gets a chance to explain.

1111111113111111121.content:
I woke up the next morning, and took a good look at how much I had already changed.

My build was now noticeably feminine. I doubted if anyone who didn’t know me would think I had been born a boy.

My breasts had grown again. "Good morning, girls," I said to them. "I think we're officially done with the training bras, don't you?"

As for my boy stuff, that was down to maybe a quarter of the size it had been. It looked like somebody had stuck a child's privates onto me.

I decided to relieve myself before I got into the shower. Given the size of what I had to work with, I decided that now was as good a time as any to start getting into the habit of peeing sitting down.

******************************


After having my hair and makeup done for me for two days in a row, I decided to give it a try myself. I didn’t go for anything as adventurous as what I got at Lady Chique, but I think I nailed the I'm-not-wearing-any-makeup look.

Downstairs, Mom was in her scrubs, which meant that she had to go back to work. "So," I asked, "what's on the agenda for today?"

"Today," Cait replied, "you're coming with us to the mall, and helping us get a handle on your personal style. Mom gave me her credit card."

"But don't go overboard," Mom said. "You're getting the clothes you'll need, not putting on a one-woman fashion show."

"Understood," I said. "Who's we?"

"You, me, and the girls. We'll help you hone your sense of style. The plan is, we'll get you some outfits, your own sleepwear, a swimsuit or two, two over-the-shoulder purses, one clutch purse, and three pairs of shoes."

"And that's not going overboard?"

"You may be surprised how much you'll really want once you get going."

After Mom left, the girls came over, and we piled into the car, with Cait driving.
1111111113111111121.title: The Next Morning

11111111131111111211.content:
"Will you be okay with this, Heather?" Becky asked. "I don't want to make this stressful for you."

"While it's happening, I can go with the flow," I replied. "I only freak out when I'm thinking about what's in the future, or when something happens that I didn't expect."

The mall was a celebration for my senses. The perfumes that would make me gag just a few weeks ago now seemed interesting to me. "Smell, but don't touch," Cait said. "Mom said I was too young for perfume when I was 14, and I don't think she's changed her mind."

I wasn't too disappointed (well, maybe a little), but the clothes took my mind off it. The fabric felt wonderful against my skin. Why didn't anyone tell me that clothes could feel like this?

Probably because I was a boy, so why would I be interested in girls' clothes?

Most of the clothes I picked out were blouses, tops, jeans, and slacks. With the help of the girls, I got them so I could mix and match, so I could go a month without repeating the same look. I also got a couple of skirts (some below the knee, some above), which I didn't think would be a frequent part of my wardrobe, but might be appropriate sometimes. I also got two dresses, one for weddings and funerals, the other for family portraits and gatherings.

Next, I got a couple of swimsuits, both one-piece. Pink didn't bother me much anymore, which was good for the robes I picked out, one for the pool or beach, the other a bathrobe.

Next was my sleepwear. I got a sleep shirt of my own, with a unicorn on it. I also got a nightie.

At the shoe store, I got three pairs: two pairs of sneakers and a pair of dress shoes.

To thank my friends for their help, we all got lunch at the food court. We had a great time, even Becky and Mel, who kept bumping their hands into each other's and blushing.
11111111131111111211.title: The Mall

111111111311111112111.content:
When we got home, Job 1 was clearing out all my boy clothes from my closet and dresser, to make room for my new wardrobe.

As we worked, Keiko asked, "How is this for you, Heather? You appear to be crying."

"I guess I am," I replied. "Though I can't put my finger on why."

"It must feel like saying goodbye to a parts of yourself."

"Just one part. And that's it. That's exactly what we're doing here: saying goodbye to John. When this is all over, John will only be in pictures and memories."

"They were happy memories," Becky said. "But I'm looking forward to making new memories with Heather."

"I'm looking forward to that, too. And, the sooner you and Mel stop staring at each other, the faster we can do that."

******************************


When Mom got home, we couldn't resist showing off my clothes in a mini fashion show. Mom and Patti enjoyed it, and even Chuck seemed to get into it, maybe imagining himself putting on his own show someday.

"That was money well spent," Mom said, when it was over. "But I'm afraid I neglected part of your education, honey."

"What part?" I asked.

"I forgot to teach you how to shave your legs and under your arms. We'll do that tomorrow morning."

"Tomorrow morning. Got it."

"I think there's something else we should do tomorrow, Mrs. Saunders," Mel said. "As we were putting Heather's clothes away, it seemed like her room was... missing something."

"You're thinking Heather needs a room that looks more like a teenage girl's room?"

"Uh-huh."

"I agree. Duncan, how's tomorrow look for you?"

"I've got nothing planned."

"Well, give the girls a hand tomorrow."

"I was thinking we could make it more of a girls-only thing," Cait said.

"You'll still need him to pay for your purchases, remember. And to give you pointers on how to get it done faster, so Heather can move back in sooner."

******************************


That night, I took another step toward my life as Heather: I changed my email address, and, after taking a selfie, updated my social profiles. Then, I sent the word to my friends.
111111111311111112111.title: Showing Off My New Clothes

1111111113111111121112.content:
The next day, after Mom taught me how to shave my underarms and legs (and I used a bunch of those little round Band-Aids on my legs), Dad took me to the home improvement store.

I picked out a bright yellow for three of the walls, with a lavender for the windowsill, door frame, and door. I also got baby blue for an accent wall. And, of course, we got primer.

As we looked at the curtains, I said, "Thanks for helping me with this, Daddy."

"That's okay, Heather," he replied. "I'm just glad I can do something for you."

"What do you mean?"

"It's not like there's much I can teach a girl."

I turned to face him, and turned him to face me. "How can you say that, Daddy? Okay, you don't know much about being a girl. But, if Cait were along with us, she'd tell you you have plenty to teach us. You're teaching us, by example, what sort of person we should look for, to make us as happy as you've made Mom."

After we picked out curtains, my attention wandered, and I started looking at the DVDs.

As I scanned the racks, somebody said, "Hey there, cutie."

I turned. There were four boys, older than I was. "What's your name?"

"None of your business."

"That's okay," another one said. "You'll be a lot friendlier when we're done." The four of them surrounded me.

"Let me through."

"We just met, sweet-cheeks. You should be more polite."

"In the first place," Dad's voice said, "she's been very polite. In the second, she's made it clear that she's not interested in you. Third, she's not even 14 yet. I should know: I'm her father."

And, just like that, I wasn't the most uncomfortable person in the area.

"Now," Dad said, "I'm sure that none of you boys want to spend the rest of your lives introducing yourselves to your neighbors as registered sex offenders. So, why don't you all find somewhere else to be. Right now."

They took the hint.

I found safety and comfort in Dad's arms.

"There are always a few wolves out there," Dad said, "looking for lost little lambs. This is why I don't let my little girls go off alone, anywhere."

"Thank you, Daddy."

"Besides , you almost missed the one thing no teenage girl's bedroom is complete without: a full-length mirror."
1111111113111111121112.title: Home Improvement

choices: Chuck and Patti had swapped clothes.
11111111131111111211121.content:
The redecoration of my bedroom began early in the morning, after breakfast. Mom was able to get the day off for the occasion. Dad was at work, which only left Chuck, who Mom cleverly had working with Patti to exchange ideas for when they swap genders.

"A little game of dress up and role play won't hurt them." Bemused, Mom said. "At least this way, they can better ease into their new roles."

"Well, let's get started then." Smiling, Cait said.

With that, she started some music, and the work began, with us all wearing sweatpants, sweatshirts, and sneakers. First, all of the furniture was carried out and the pictures were taken down. I began to cry a little at the sight of the empty room, until Keiko said, "Think of it as an empty canvas; what image will you paint?"

I smiled at that and replied, "Bright yellow for three of the walls, lavender for the windowsill, door frame, and door.. and baby blue for an accent wall."

"Sounds like a plan." With a wink, Becky said.

"Brushes up." Smiling, Mel added.

The two tapped their brushes together as if they were wine glasses, blushing. If there was a single more obvious sign they were interested in each other, I didn't know of it. But, before things go too awkward, I took up my brush and held it up. "Wait for the rest of us, you two."

Mel and Becky blushed more, but smiled as the rest of us took up a brush and tapped them all together, before grabbing plastic and paper to begin painting. Becky and Mel took on the windowsill, door, and door frame. Keiko and Mom took on the three walls, and I alone worked on the accent wall. It was something to link myself to the boy I once was, so it was personal to me. "Another step towards a new chapter in my life." Whispering, I said. "A flowering evergreen on the peaty barren lands... "

"The meaning of your name." Nodding, Mom said. "It's the symbol of life, hope, and new beginnings."

"It suits me, I think." Smiling, I said.

"Yes, it does." Smiling back, Mom agreed.

By the time lunch time had rolled around, the room had been completely painted and was drying. "I haven't heard a word from the twins in hours." Worried, I said.

"Let's check up on them." Sighing, Mom said.

We all went to Patti's room, where they had decided to play. Upon looking inside, everyone had a bemused grin on their faces, as they saw...
11111111131111111211121.title: The Girls Redo My Room

111111111311111112111211.content:
Chuck was wearing Patti's jumper and blouse, playing with Barbie and Ken. Patti, for her part, was wearing Chuck's T-shirt and jeans, playing with Chuck's cars. Only their hairstyles suggested that there was something unusual about the proceedings.

"Are you having fun?" Mom asked.

"Lots," Chuck replied. "I can't believe I used to think these were boring."

"Same here," Patti added. "Can we do this again, Mom?"

"Of course, sweetie," Mom promised. "You can do this as many times as you'd like."

"But you may want to be particular about who sees you like this," Cait said. "Some kids are more understanding than others."

Chuck nodded. "A few of the guys asked me if it was true my brother was a freak."

"And what did you say?" I asked.

"I told them no. I mean, I don't have a brother. Not yet, anyway. But I didn't tell them that last part. I just told them I had a cool big sister."

A cool big sister, I thought. "I'll take that, Chuck. Or should I call you Patti, when you're dressed like this?"

Chuck shook his head. "We're not going to turn into each other. We'll pick out our own names. We just don't know what they'll be, yet."

"You've got years before it becomes a pressing concern," Mom said. "Plenty of time to whittle down a list of names."

"How can you tell?" Patti asked.

"Because I whittled two lists of names down to four before you two were born. And I only had five or six months. It may seem daunting, but I've done it four times." She gave Patti and Chuck a hug. "You've had something big dropped onto your plates, but you're holding up admirably. I'm sure you two will be fine."
111111111311111112111211.title: Chuck and Patti had swapped clothes.

1111111113111111121112111.content:
It was the morning of the day before my 14th birthday. After my shower and shaving, I stood naked in front of my mirror, turning this way and that. I wasn't completely female yet, but it wouldn't be long now.

My breasts had filled out nicely, easily filling a B-cup bra. My butt was equally shapely, giving people behind me something to look at as it wiggled when I walked.

As for my genitalia, there wasn't much there there. My penis was almost completely gone, with just a bit sticking out. My vulva had taken shape, but was only skin deep.

Still, it was easy to tell that, in a little less than 24 hours, Heather Marie Saunders would be 100% girl. And, after all the changes I had already been through, I didn't mind a bit.

******************************


All my friends came over, and they were as excited for my future as I was. Dan, in particular, couldn't take his eyes off me, and I really didn't mind that much. I wanted him to look, and even to like what he saw. Part of me even imagined him putting his hands on me,... but I'd better not let myself think about that too much.

"So," Cait asked, "what's on the agenda for today?"

"Not much," I replied. "Hanging out. Learn a new song on the guitar, maybe."

"Have me drag you around playing video games?" Dan asked.

I chuckled. "The only way you're dragging me around, is if I kick your butt so hard that my foot gets stuck there."

"Before you start with that," Becky said, "I'd like to invite the girls to a sleepover at my house tonight. We'll play some great games - don't worry, Keiko, they're easy to catch on to - and have a great time."

"Sounds good to me," I replied.

"Instead," Mom said, "let's do it here. That way, Patti can join in."

We all agreed.

"And now," I said, "It's time to beat Dan at some games."
1111111113111111121112111.title: Almost Done Changing

11111111131111111211121111.content:
By the time the sleepover came, I felt a blossoming within me, and something opening up inside me. There was no doubt in my mind that I was now completely female. It felt surreal, though part of me felt a certain sadness. As I gazed at my reflection, I could feel tears running down my face. I barely heard Mom knock before she entered and gave me a warm hug from behind.

"You're entering a new chapter of your life, Heather." Softly, she said. "A world that would have otherwise remained a complete mystery awaits you to explore it."

I smiled at that, and turned to return her hug. "Thanks, Mom."

"Our guests should be here any minute." Smiling, Mom said. "Dad is taking Chuck and Dan out camping tonight, so it's just us girls here. Finish getting ready; it's going to be a fun night."

Becky, Melanie, and Keiko arrived minutes later, and we all gathered in the spacious living room to start our sleepover. Patti was ready for the event, wearing her favorite night clothes and carrying her teddy bear. "What are we going to do first?" Excited, she asked. "Makeovers? Movie... ?"

"Pizza and soda." Grinning, Mom said. "After that, we'll get to the fun and games."

"So, how do you feel, now that you're fully female?" Curious, Mel asked.

"It doesn't feel as strange as I imagined it would." Honestly, I replied.

Becky giggled. "Well, you look great." Smiling, she said.

"Thanks." Smiling back, I replied.

"Okay, first game." Grinning, Mom announced. "Truth or Dare."

The girls giggled, though Cait had a grin of our own. "Be fair warned." Teasing, she said, "Mom is a pro at this game."

"I remember." Blushing, Mel said. "We'll have to be careful how we answer when she picks one of us."

"You know the rules." Sitting down, Mom said. "No gossiping about the truths revealed here tonight, and no extreme dares." She grinned. "I go first... "

Who does she pick?
11111111131111111211121111.title: The Sleepover

111111111311111112111211111.content:
"Heather," Mom said, "Truth or Dare?"

I gulped, and decided to rip off the Band-Aid. "Dare."

"It's time for us to see the new you, Heather: Take off your clothes."

That didn't sound too bad. After all, we were all girls. I took off my nightie and panties, then stood up and turned around, letting everyone see.

As I got dressed again, it was Cait's turn. "Keiko: Truth or Dare?"

Keiko swallowed. "Truth."

"Who was your first kiss?"

"I do not know. Except for my parents, I have not been kissed."

We all hugged her. "We'll find you a boy to kiss, Keiko," Mel said. "Or a girl."

Keiko smiled. "A boy would be preferable."

"My turn," I said. "Becky: Truth or Dare?"

"Dare."

"You've pussyfooted your way around this for the last time, Becky Buckley. Kiss Mel on the lips."

Becky blinked.

The other girls, having picked up on the same signals I had, began chanting, "Kiss! Kiss! Kiss!"

Finally, Becky crawled across the floor, wrapped her arms around Mel, and kissed her.

After the cheering died down, Mel said, "Heather: Truth or Dare?"

"Truth."

"Do you like Dan, or do you like him?"

"We played some video games this afternoon, and I let him win a couple of times. Then, I worked on a new song on my guitar. I messed up a few times, but Dan told me he thought I was doing well. Then he kissed me on the forehead. But I wish... I wish he had kissed me on the lips, instead."

Then, it was Patti's turn. "Keiko: Truth or Dare?"

"Truth."

"What were you like in Japan?"

It was an innocuous question, but Keiko looked as if she'd been backed into a corner.

Finally, she said, "There is not much to tell. I did not have many friends, and I left my house only when I had to go. I was a shy boy."

"I know you have problems with American idioms," Becky said. "But don't you mean you were a shy girl?"

"No, I do not," Keiko replied. "A few months before we moved to America, I changed, just as Heather did."
111111111311111112111211111.title: Me.

choices: he packed a nightgown without thinking.
he dreamed that he was changing early.
1111111113111111121112111111.content:
Everyone in the room grew wide-eyed for a moment, then teared up, me and Patti most of all. Keiko winced, fearing what we would do, but teared up herself as I pulled her into a warm hug, with Patti and the others quickly following in a group hug, supporting Keiko.

"I'm glad we became friends." Kindly, I said.

"Me too." Smiling, Patti said.

The others voiced their agreement and shared a group hug for a moment, as Keiko smiled, a weight seemingly lifted off her shoulders. "Thank you, all of you."

"I wondered how you seemed to know what Heather was going through." Kindly, Mel said. "It was because you had already gone through it yourself."

Keiko nodded. "With time, I was able to accept who I had become." Rubbing her tears, she said. "Only not all people are as understanding... "

"Which was why you moved to America." Guessing, Becky said.

"One of the reasons." Nodding, Keiko replied.

"That's in the past." Firmly but kindly, Mom said. "As I told Heather, you are now exploring a world that would otherwise remain forever a mystery." She smiled, motioning to me. "Now, you can help lead your friend into that world, with all that you had learned."

Keiko smiled at that. "I would be honored."

"You aren't doing it alone." smiling, Mel said. "We girls have to stick together."

"I won't be a girl after a while." Suddenly sad, Patti said.

"Maybe, but you are a girl right now." Pulling her into a hug, Becky said. "For now, you're our toughest little tomboy."

"To us girls." Raising a cup of soda, Mom said.

Everyone paused then giggled, raising their own soda cups. "To us girls!"

The rest of the sleepover went smoothly, with everyone having a good time. I slept well that night, knowing I had a friend who knew what I had gone through. In a way, I believed she felt the same. I promised myself to be there when it came Patti and Chuck's turn to change.

******************************


Mom was still awake when Dad returned home early morning with Chuck, who seemed to have been crying for a while. "What happened?" Worried, Mom asked.

Dad sighed. "It's nothing serious." Bemused, he replied. "Chuck is upset because... "
1111111113111111121112111111.title: Reactions

11111111131111111211121111111.content:
"... he accidentally packed a nightgown for the trip."

Mom mussed his hair. "It's alright, Chuck. We all know about it. You're going to change in a few years, just as Heather changed. And you're curious about what it's like. So you're getting in touch with your feminine side, just like Patti is becoming a tomboy. You're going to be okay, son."

******************************


The morning was a big one for me: not only was it my birthday, it was my first full day of being a girl.

I got curious about what my new anatomy was like, so I picked up my tablet and looked up female genitalia. Then, I took a handheld mirror and held it to give myself a look, comparing mine with the illustration. No difference.

There was a knock at my door, and I self-consciously covered myself and put aside the mirror and tablet.

Cait stuck her head in. "Happy birthday, little sister," she said. "Mom's making pancakes, with blueberries for you." Then she looked at my bed. "Checking out the merchandise?"

Busted. "I... I... "

She came in. "It's okay, sis. I did the same thing when I was your age, and my equipment came factory standard."

"You... you did?"

She nodded, picking up the mirror. "With this very mirror. Every girl is curious about her body. So why should you be any different?"

"You mean it's normal?"

"It's normal. You're normal. The way you got here wasn't the usual route, but you're normal, just the same. So, got any questions?"

I shook my head. "I can't think of any."

"You will. And when you do, ask them. You can ask Mom, or me, or Becky, or Keiko. We'll give you answers."
11111111131111111211121111111.title: he packed a nightgown without thinking.

111111111311111112111211111111.content:
After the breakfast dishes had been taken care of, Mom said, "You girls will need time to get the party set up, and I've got just the thing: Heather, I got you in to see our gynecologist."

"Do I need to do anything in advance?"

"No, just come as you are. I will forewarn you, the speculum will feel strange, but it's not going to hurt."

******************************


Well, Mom didn't lie. But Dr. George was very friendly. She walked me through everything, and declared that I was anatomically no different than if I'd been born a girl. She also gave me some pamphlets so I could read about what was in store for me as a teenage girl.

When we got home, the decorations were in place. Pink balloons announced the occasion to passersby, and the living room was festooned with streamers. I was greeted with everyone singing "Happy Birthday", and then it happened.

Dan kissed me. On the mouth.

When I recovered the ability to speak, I said, "Thank you."

"I had a dream last night," he said. "You went off with some guy, because he kissed you before I could work up the courage. I certainly didn't want that to happen."

I didn't care. I kissed him back.

My party was light on presents, since everyone only had a week to get ready for me to be a girl. Mom did get me my first diary, though. Just the thing for recording my innermost thoughts, while the lock would keep prying eyes out.
111111111311111112111211111111.title: My Birthday Party

1111111113111111121112111111111.content:
After the party was over, I was stretched out on my bed, reading a pamphlet on periods, when Mom knocked on my door. "Got a minute?"

"Got a lot of them," I replied. "What's up?"

"Nothing major. I just thought it was about time for me to give you The Talk." I swear, I could hear the capital letters.

I pulled myself into a sitting position, and Mom sat down next to me. "Let's start with some of the things you'll need." She handed me a Walgreens bag. "Pads and tampons. The pads go into your panties, and the tampons are inserted. You might find that the pads make you walk funny, and you can't go swimming with them. On the other hand, you might have some trepidation about inserting a tampon. Whichever way you choose, that's the right way for you. But the pads will be better for when you're sleeping, since the tampons have to be changed every three or four hours.

"Now, I assume you've been relieving yourself as necessary? Well, you have to be sure to wipe when you're done, so you don't get an infection. And wipe front to back, so you don't get fecal matter into your vagina."

"Yeah," I said, "that would be bad."

Mom nodded. "Now, advertising will try to sell you douche, but you really shouldn't need it. Your vagina can clean itself. The pink tax is bad enough, you shouldn't spend more money on something you don't need."

"Pink tax?"

"Women's products are more expensive than men's. Since the only difference is the color pink, it's called the pink tax.

"Now that we've taken care of your hygiene, it's time to move on to other matters. In the bag, you'll find a canister of pepper spray. Carry it with you at all times. It's better to have it and not need it than to need it and not have it. Also, if possible, don't go anywhere alone at night. If you have to, walk with your keys in between your fingers, like this." She demonstrated, her keys looking like Wolverine's claws.

"Mom, you're scaring me."

"It's a scary world. Now, I know the school taught you where babies come from. Now, I'm going to make you think about where they come out."

And she did. She told me everything she felt when she was pregnant with me, and how long she was in labor. Then she told me the things she'd seen patients go through. And then she rattled off a list of sexually transmitted infections, and their effects on the human body.

By the time she was done, I didn't know why anybody ever had sex, let alone got pregnant.
1111111113111111121112111111111.title: The Talk

11111111131111111211121111111111.content:
The next morning, I was feeling good. I had a radio playing as I showered, and I was singing along with Taylor Swift.

My mood shifted, however, when I opened the shower curtain, and I saw Chuck standing right in front of me. Hell yes, I screamed when I saw him.

Cait was the first to get there. "What happened?" she asked. "See a spider?" Then she saw Chuck. "What the hell are you doing here?"

Then Mom showed up. "What's going on?"

"Chuck decided to do a little sightseeing," Cait explained.

"Let's go back to your room, young man," Mom said. Turning to me, she added, "You can join us later, if you want."

Once I was alone in the bathroom again, I stepped out to finish my morning routine.

By the time I was ready to get dressed, it occurred to me that Chuck was probably curious, particularly since he would be changing in a few years. He probably didn't mean any harm, but he still needed to have boundaries set, especially before he could try it on someone who didn't know what he was going through.

Once I was dressed, I went to Chuck's room. He was sitting on the bed while Mom lectured him. Mom and Dad were firm believers in the idea that, if you couldn't find a nonviolent way to teach children right from wrong, then maybe you didn't understand it yourself. Accordingly, they always explained to us why what we did was wrong.

It was clear that Chuck was taking the lesson to heart. When I came to the door, the first thing he said was, "I'm sorry, Heather."

"Apology accepted," I replied. "I can't forget what you did, but I can forgive. You understand why it was wrong?"

He nodded. "You wanted to be alone, and I should have left you alone. I guess I was wondering what I would look like, when it happens to me."

"Curiosity is a fine thing," Mom said. "Intruding on others is not. We should find some way to answer your questions that doesn't involve walking in on girls when they aren't dressed."

"How about these?" Cait asked.

She handed us her tablet, where she had found two books: It's Perfectly Normal, by Robie H. Harris, and Sex, Puberty, and all That Stuff, by Jacqui Bailey. Both had illustrations, so he could look at girls' bodies without intruding on them.

"These could work," Mom said. "And they're about growing up, so having them won't get you too much attention."

"Okay," Chuck said.

"And share them with Patti. She hasn't walked in on anyone, but I'm sure she's just as curious."
11111111131111111211121111111111.title: A Surprise

111111111311111112111211111111111.content:
I had been a girl for about a week, and that morning didn't start my way.

I was awakened by cramps in my lower abdomen. Not horrible, but bad enough to get my attention. "Hope this isn't serious," I said.

Rolling over, I found that my breasts were sore, now, too. "What is wrong with me?"

Cait knocked on my door. "How you doing, sis?"

"Get Mom," I said, "I'm sick."

Mom came a few minutes later. "What's wrong, honey?"

"I don't know. Could... could I be changing back?"

"Well, Dr. Ramsey says there's no changing back, so I doubt it. Why don't you tell me what you're feeling, and we'll see if we can figure out what's going on?"

"My tummy hurts, and my boobs are sore."

Mom nodded. "Lie on your back."

When I did, she put her hand on my stomach, just under my ribs. "Here?"

I shook my head. "Lower."

"How far?"

"Under my belly button."

She nodded again. "Well, I've got a hunch."

"What is it?"

"You've got PMS."

"Dammit."

Mom gave me some Midol, and Cait brought me a glass of water. "How bad are the cramps, on a scale of 1 to 10?"

"About a 3."

"Some light exercise will help. I know, it's the last thing you feel like doing, but trust me. You may also feel bloated. That's your tissues retaining water instead of sending it to your bladder. The Midol will help with that, too.

"Now, you may experience some mood swings. One moment, you want to curl up and cry your eyes out; the next, it's like everyone is getting on your last nerve. And you won't even know why. I wish there was a magic pill that would take care of it, but there isn't. All you can do is try to avoid too much stress, and don't act on those impulses."

I sighed, hoping my PMS wouldn't be too bad.
111111111311111112111211111111111.title: PMS

1111111113111111121112111111111111.content:
Mom was right about the bloating: all of my clothes seemed to fit wrong, like I was putting on weight. If I didn't know what was going on, I might have ripped something to shreds in frustration. Forewarned, however, I trusted the Midol to ease my water retention.

My mood swings still caught me by surprise, though. When I walked out of my room, I wound up bumping into Patti, and both of us fell to the floor. "Sorry," she said, a giggle in her voice.

I, however, was in no mood for giggles. "Why don't you look where you're going?"

"I was," she replied.

That set me off even more. "Don't talk back, brat. You're no good for anything."

Patti didn't say anything. She just looked at my face, and began to cry.

When she did, my mood swung again, and I hugged her, doing some crying of my own. "I'm sorry, Patti. I shouldn't have said that. You're not good for nothing, I'm good for nothing."

We sat there for a while, crying on each other's shoulders, until Chuck came along. "What did you do," he asked, "to make Patti cry?"

That just made us cry more.

Finally, Mom came back up. "Can someone tell me what the yelling is about?"

Between sobs, I told her.

She rubbed my back. "Those mood swings pack a punch, don't they?"

I nodded.

******************************


"How long is Heather going to be like this?" Chuck asked.

"Three or four days is my experience," Cait answered. "And then, after a month, she'll have to go through it again."

"You mean put us through it," he countered.

"Trust me, Chuck," Mom said, "Heather is just as much a prisoner of this. Watch and learn, because you'll have to go through it, too."

"Seems like he's already going through it," Patti said.

"No, that's a different phenomenon," Dad said. "Male hormone levels fluctuate throughout the day, and it can put us on edge, too. Watch and learn, Patti, since that's in your future."

"Can we do anything to make Heather feel better?"

"Well, what sets her off at this time is something that bothers her the rest of the month. She's just not in the mood to let it slide. So, if she complains about something, let's do our best to stop doing that. As for the rest, all we can do is ride it out."

"Like a hurricane," Patti said.

"Yeah," Chuck said. "Hurricane Heather."

Little did I know that name was going to stick.
1111111113111111121112111111111111.title: The Rise of "Hurricane Heather"

11111111131111111211121111111111111.content:
I was lying in a lounge chair by the pool, trying to relax, when the girls came over. "Hey, girlfriend," Mel said. "Whatcha doing?"

"Avoiding people," I replied, "or trying to. It's safer that way."

"Heather," Keiko asked, "what is the matter? We are your friends, we want to help."

"PMS is the matter," I replied. "Midol does a good job with the cramps and the bloating, but my moods are all over the map. I didn't want to hurt my family any more than I already have, and I don't want to hurt you, either."

"And, with that, I must tap out of the discussion," Mel said. "The closest I get to PMS is a therapy-induced craving for pickles."

"I've got something you can try, Heather," Becky said. She reached into her clutch purse, pulled something out, and put it in my hand.

I looked at it. "A candy bar?"

"Chocolate always makes me feel better. Give it a try."

I thought about it. If it didn't work, I would at least have a candy bar with my friends. I unwrapped the bar, broke off a piece, and ate it. "Here goes nothing."

Keiko turned to Mel. "You crave pickles? Why?"

"HRT takes out a lot of salt," she explained. "Pickles are soaked in salt water. Thus, I love eating pickles."

"Well, I'm glad they help you, Mel," I said. "Here's hoping that chocolate helps me." I broke off another piece.

"How bad are the mood swings?"

"I go from bawling my eyes out to lashing out at anyone nearby. This morning, I said some pretty mean things to Patti. When she started crying, I started crying. Chuck compares me to a hurricane."

"Well," Becky said, "if you are a hurricane, I think we've found the eye of the storm."

She was right. I hadn't shed a single tear. Not for Mel's need for pickles, not for thinking about how much I lashed out. "Let's hope it stays that way. Candy bars and M&Ms are easy to fit into a purse."

"Or a bug-out bag," Mel said, "if that real-life hurricane heads our way."

"What is a hurricane?" Keiko asked.

"It's the same thing as a typhoon," Becky replied. "What are they calling this one?"

Mel smiled. "Hurricane Heather."
11111111131111111211121111111111111.title: Hanging Out

111111111311111112111211111111111111.content:
A couple days later, we got official word that the meteorological phenomenon that was Hurricane Heather was expected to at least clip West Palm Beach. Dad put plywood over the windows, and the rest of us packed our most important things that we would take to my aunt and uncle's house in Orlando. We usually did it at least once a year, so we all knew what to do.

This year, however, three things were different: Patti and Chuck were occasionally practicing being members of the opposite sex, and I was PMSing. The possible fate of everything I couldn't carry brought tears to my eyes, as did the worry that I was slowing the family down. Finally, I just had to take a chocolate break, before my tears threatened to make my namesake even worse. Once I had a little of what made Hershey, Pennsylvania, famous in my system, I was able to focus on the task at hand.

Finished, I brought my two suitcases downstairs, where I was relieved to discover that I hadn't hopelessly slowed the family down. I helped Patti pack (mostly by letting her decide which of her boy things she was ready to explain), and we gathered in the family room.

Dad came in. "Alright, Saunders family," he said, "we've got just enough time for everybody to go before we leave. We want to get well out of town before we even think of making a pit stop."

Thus warned, we each picked one of the three bathrooms in the house to do our business in. I managed to beat Cait to the upstairs john, and I dropped trou and assumed the position.

As nature took its course, I happened to look at my panties. Was that... ?

"Cait? Can you come in a sec?"

She came in. "What's the matter," she asked, "that can't wait until we... "

I showed her my panties. "Is it... ?"

She nodded. "It sure is. Welcome to womanhood, Heather." She poked her head out the door. "We've got to delay departure, Daddy."

"What on Earth for?" Dad called back. "Can't it possibly wait?"

"Don't blame me, Daddy, blame Aunt Flo."

Mom's voice came next. "Heather?"

"Right here, Mom. Unfortunately, all the stuff I need is packed."

"There should be some in the drawer."

Cait opened the drawer and pulled out a tampon. Scanning the directions, I unwrapped it and inserted it. Once it was in, I didn't feel it.

After that, I cleaned myself up, got dressed (in clean underwear), and went back downstairs, where I was greeted with a big hug and kiss from Mom.

As we got underway, it occurred to me that this would be the first time anyone in my extended family saw the new me. How would they react? Would anything be different, now that I was a young woman instead of a boy?
111111111311111112111211111111111111.title: The Hurricane Is Coming

1111111113111111121112111111111111111.content:
Despite my very first period, we were still able to get out of town before the mass exodus caused bottlenecks on every thoroughfare. Relaxing, we passed the time the way we usually did: family sing-alongs, with the occasional request from Patti or Chuck that we stop for ice cream or miniature golf. Dad always said no to these requests, not stopping until we were closer to Orlando.

Finally, once we got into town, Dad pulled into a McDonald's, and we all ordered lunch and sat down. This was all part of our routine, and we were glad for the fresh air, and to see something from outside the minivan. And to make one last pit stop before we got to Aunt Rachel and Uncle Jim's.

******************************


Aunt Rachel and Uncle Jim always came out to greet us as soon as we arrived. This year, Aunt Rachel made a bigger fuss over me. "There she is," she said, hugging me. "You look so beautiful, my dear."

"She gets it from her mother," Dad added. Nobody disagreed, since Uncle Jim was Mom's brother.

"How are you feeling, Heather?" Aunt Rachel asked.

"Pretty good," I replied. "I got my period right before we left."

This brought on another hug. "You're a woman, already. I'll bet you didn't think it would be like this, did you?"

"Pretty safe bet," Uncle Jim said, "considering how she started out."

Their attention shifted to Patti and Chuck. In previous years, Patti got attention from Aunt Rachel, and Chuck from Uncle Jim. This year, however, Aunt Rachel hugged Chuck, and Uncle Jim hugged Patti.

"Your mother told us the news," Aunt Rachel said. "You'll be a beautiful girl, Chuck."

"And you'll be a big, strong boy, Patti," Uncle Jim said, "just like your uncle."

With their greetings out of the way, we all went inside, to meet our cousins. One look at their faces told me that they weren't sure how to make heads or tails out of me, or, to a lesser extent, Patti and Chuck. After all, last Thanksgiving, none of us had changed our sex, or been told that it would happen.

Finally, it was Chrissie who made the first move. About the same age as Cait, she came to Cait and me and said, "I guess you two will be with me. Follow me."

Aunt Rachel and Uncle Jim had the largest family on this side of our family tree. After Chrissie, there was Mitch, Karen, Jena, Shaun, and baby Ian. Previously, I had bunked with Mitch, but that obviously wouldn't work anymore. Patti would be with Karen and Jena, and Chuck would be with Shaun.

As Cait and I put away our things, I asked, "How are things, now that Ian is here?" Aunt Rachel had still been pregnant with him on Thanksgiving.

"As to be expected," Chrissie replied. "When Mom's busy, it's my job to help with him."
