"Well, you'll have help with him for the next few days," Cait said. "I'll be glad to help take care of him."

"Me, too," I said.

"I can't get over how much you've changed, what's happened to you," Chrissie said. "How much of a girl are you?"

"Three words: Dracula's tea bags," I replied, bringing a fresh tampon into Chrissie's bathroom.
1111111113111111121112111111111111111.title: My Aunt and Uncle's House

11111111131111111211121111111111111111.content:
For supper, Aunt Rachel and Uncle Jim ordered pizza. The cousins relaxed when I asked for my favorite, since it hadn't changed, proving I was still the same person they knew all these years.

"What's your period like for you, Heather?" Chrissie asked.

Before I could answer, I was hit with horrible pain. It felt like a bomb went off inside me. I collapsed, using words that I'm sure nobody wanted children to hear.

Mom came right to me. "Heather? What is it, baby? What's wrong?"

"Cramps," I croaked. "Hurts."

"On a scale from 1 to 10,... "

"12!"

Mom called a number and put her phone on speaker. "I just hope we can reach them."

Somebody picked up. "Dr. Ramsey."

"You're there!" Mom said. " I didn't think anybody would be in, what with the hurricane."

"Not at all. I just transferred the calls to my cell. What seems to be the matter?"

"Heather's having painful cramps. I don't know why, or what to do for her."

"Let me guess: Is Heather having her period?"

"How did you know?"

"Heather changed rather late, after going through male puberty. We don't know why, but such girls have excruciating cramps during their first periods. It's as painful as childbirth."

Just what I needed: another reason not to look forward to having children.

"Isn't there something I can do for her?"

"Tell me your pharmacy, and where you are now. I can prescribe some pain medication for her. She won't be dancing, but it should take care of the pain."

"And I've got to take it all my life?" I asked.

"Now, there, I have good news: It should ease up after a few months. You probably won't have to deal with it when school starts."

"Thank you, Doctor." Mom gave her the information she asked for.

"Your prescription should be ready for pick-up tomorrow. And, when you can, bring the twins in. We may be able to prevent them from going through this."
11111111131111111211121111111111111111.title: Bad News at Supper

choices: I talk to Chuck and Patti.
111111111311111112111211111111111111111.content:
That night, I was a hair-trigger away from being furious or in tears due to my painful cramps. I locked myself in the bedroom, to avoid the family having to deal with two hurricanes. Still, I felt bad for Patti and Chuck, who I heard on and off trying to pick new names for themselves.

"How about Patrick?" Stress in her voice, Patti asked. "I'm always called Pat for short."

"If you really want." Honestly, Chuck replied. "How about Charlie for me?"

"Hm... sounds a bit boyish. Maybe... Cassie?"

Chuck sighed heavily. "I don't know... "

I winced, listening to them. They were scared, and I really couldn't blame them. I had decided to step out and try to assure them any way I could, when I saw Uncle Jim approach them. I pulled back the door to listen and watch.

"Don't worry about the past and don't fear the future, as the present is the only time you have to act." Calmly, Uncle Jim said. He ruffled up their hair, causing them both to chuckle. "Enjoy being Chuck and Patti while you can. You have each other to help out when your time comes, okay?"

Chuck and Patti both nodded, their smiles returning. "Okay!"

Uncle Jim then turned in my direction, causing me to pause. "You were a boy once." Still calm, he said. "You know what it's like to be kicked in the jewels."

This took me aback. "Um... yeah." Not knowing where this was going, I replied.

Uncle Jim nodded. "It hurts... all up into your chest, causing your legs to buckle, and you want to scream every obscenity you ever learned... " He paused, a small grin on his face. "... just like menstrual cramps."

"I... I suppose." Wincing, I said.

"Think of this as a kick in the jewels." Serious, Uncle Jim said. "Not to sound like I'm being insensitive, but there's nothing you can do but ride it out and walk it off. Locking yourself away will not teach anyone what sets you off, so it really solves nothing."

With that, he calmly walked away, leaving not only me, but Chuck and Patti, a lot to think about.

Good advice. Smiling slightly, I thought.
111111111311111112111211111111111111111.title: Good advice.

1111111113111111121112111111111111111111.content:
I walked out to the family room, though it wasn't easy. Every slight bounce put me in agony. Just like a kick in the jewels, I thought. Uncle Jim's analogy was spot on.

Aunt Rachel noticed me first. "Hello, Heather," she said. "Feeling better?"

"Not at all," I replied. "But pitying myself isn't going to help any, either."

The kids made room on the couch, and I sat down. "What's on?" I asked. "Anything interesting?"

"Watching the storm coming in," Uncle Jim replied. "The wind's starting to pick up in West Palm Beach. Looks like you'll miss the worst of it."

"Still wouldn't want to be dealing with this there. Any idiots yet?"

"Not in West Palm Beach," Dad said. "Not yet, anyway. Some guy in Miami decided this was a great time to break out the parachute."

I shook my head and giggled. "It just wouldn't be a hurricane without Florida Man, would it?"

"Is it that bad?" Chuck asked.

"Some hurricanes are stronger than others," I replied. "But it's foolish to deliberately mess with one. When you know we get hurricanes, you take steps to mitigate the damage, and you stay out of their way, if you can." I pointed to the TV, where they were showing the parachutist. "Not everyone can get away, but acting the fool just puts you in danger. And then people all over will laugh at you."

And then Karen asked, "Are we talking about the storm, or are we talking about something else?"

I smiled. "Let's just say that this isn't the first time we've discussed 'Hurricane Heather' in our family."
1111111113111111121112111111111111111111.title: Rejoining the family.

1111111113111111121112111111111111111111.content:
Day 2 of my first period, and Day 2 of our stay with relatives.

I got more sleep than I expected to, given the thermonuclear explosions that seemed to be going on in my uterus. I cleaned myself up, made myself presentable, and joined the rest of the family.

When I got to the breakfast table, all my cousins hugged and kissed me. If they were unsure of how to deal with a boy turned girl yesterday, they had decided today that I was a cousin who wasn't feeling well, and it was their job to cheer me up.

"Just let me eat, okay?" I said. "I'm irritable enough with my cramps and being tired from not enough sleep. But I'm hungry, too, and at least that's something I can take care of myself."

With some of Aunt Rachel's cooking in my belly, I was ready for conversation. The other Hurricane Heather had hit West Palm Beach, or at least the edge of it did. And a local Florida Man decided to try sailing down the street.

As soon as she could, Mom went to get my painkillers. The relief was so welcome that I didn't mind having to take a midday nap.
1111111113111111121112111111111111111111.title: The Next Day

1111111113111111121112111111111111111111.content:
I awoke from my nap to what must have been the usual controlled chaos of the house. Aunt Rachel was keeping the younger kids entertained, while the older ones entertained themselves.

From another room, I heard Ian crying. "Can I get some help here?" Aunt Rachel asked.

"I'm closer to him, Aunt Rachel," I said. "I'll see what I can do."

I went into the nursery and picked Ian up. A sniff was enough to tell me what was troubling him. "Let's get you cleaned up there, little guy," I said.

A scan of the room let me see where to find everything I would need for the task at hand. Laying Ian on the changing table, I got him cleaned up and put him on a clean diaper.

And he peed right in my face.

If I thought he understood, I would have been more cautious with my language. As it was, I said exactly what was on my mind.

"Did he get you?" Aunt Rachel asked. "I'm sorry, I should have warned you."

I looked at Ian's laughing face. "Does he do that on purpose?"

Aunt Rachel gave me a sly smile. "You tell me, honey: you did the same thing to me, the first time I changed your diaper."

******************************


Later in the day, Ian was hungry, in addition to having a dirty diaper. Having learned my lesson, I put a clean diaper over him as I worked. Once that was taken care of, I gave him his bottle, sat in the rocking chair, and rocked him as he ate.

Looking at the absolute contentment in his face, I thought to myself, This is nice. I wonder if it will be this way with my own children someday?

And I realized that, for the first time since I found out what was happening to me, the thought of becoming a mother one day didn't terrify me.
1111111113111111121112111111111111111111.title: Ian

1111111113111111121112111111111111111111.content:
It was fun staying with family for a few days, even with pain meds that had me taking naps. But the hurricane passed, and it was time for us to go back home. We packed our bags, and, after hugs and kisses, we headed out.

"How much fun did you two kids have?" Cait asked Patti and Chuck.

"It was okay," Patti said. "Nobody minded that I like to dress like a boy sometimes."

"They didn't mind me dressing like a girl, either," Chuck added. "I just wish I could have come closer to picking out my new name."

"You've got plenty of time," I replied. "Years."

"There aren't a lot of good girl names that sound like Chuck."

"So? Heather doesn't sound like John. You've got, like, every name in the book available to you. You'll find the name that's right for you."

Once we were back in West Palm Beach, we hit the drive-thru, since none of us would feel like cooking as soon as we got home.

At home, we put our bags in our rooms, then Cait and I helped Dad take down the plywood.

After we ate, Dan and the girls came over. It felt good to see Dan again. Almost as good as it felt to wrap my arms around him and kiss him.
1111111113111111121112111111111111111111.title: Going Home

12.content:
In retrospect, I should have been more concerned when I noticed my body going through some unusual changes. It all started about three weeks before the last day of 8th Grade. My sister, Caitlin, had taken her sweet time in the bathroom, leaving me barely 20 minutes to do my thing before Charlie and Patty woke up.

I was in the shower when I noticed the first sign of things to come; my face was baby smooth. That was strange, since yesterday I’d had a respectable amount of peach fuzz on my face. Oh well, guess I don’t have to worry about shaving today.

The next sign came when I was washing my hair – my hair, which typically stopped just above my ears, had grown a good inch since last night. I thought that was weird, since I’d had a haircut only a few days earlier, but my folks said my body would do weird things during puberty.


Boy, were they right.

******************************


I had an embarrassing moment at school when my voice cracked during the Pledge. A lot of my classmates, including Dan, thought it was funny when my voice stayed that way throughout the morning.

I didn’t get any peace from the teasing until lunch, when I joined Becky and Dan at our usual table. Becky hadn’t heard about my higher voice yet, until I greeted her as I sat down. Between chuckles, she said, “Wow John, your voice sounds higher now than it did before it cracked.” Looking at me, she said, “Are you feeling okay?”

“I’m fine. I guess my body is just going through some changes.” Biting my sandwich, I said, “I’m sure everything will return to normal soon enough."

Oh, how I wished that was true.
12.title: A Harbinger of Things to Come

121.content:
My voice fluctuated for a few days; at its worst, I spent most of Saturday sounding almost identical to Caitlin. Thankfully, that only lasted a day before getting lower again, although not as low as before.

If I thought sounding like my sister was embarrassing, then I was in for a rude awakening at school on Monday. It all started during Gym class; the teacher had us pair off to do trust falls. Normally we’d pair off however we liked: I usually paired up with Becky. But that changed after some kid in an earlier class got a little handsy with a girl who’d developed early.

So that’s how I ended up with Collin Barlow, the son of a local businessman, who thought he was God’s gift to girls. I know a lot of my male classmates hated him, but the same couldn’t be said for my female classmates – Becky included.

Everything went fine for the first couple falls: I caught him, and he caught me. Things changed when it came time for my third turn. As I’d done twice already, I leaned back and fell into Collin’s arms. Only this time, he had trouble catching me, and the two of us landed in a heap on the floor.

I thought nothing of it until Collin jumped off me, his face red from embarrassment, “What the hell, Saunders?”

I had no idea what was going on as the entire class gathered around me. That was until I looked down, and saw the telltale signs of an erection in my pants. I scrambled to my feet and ran towards the locker room as some of my classmates laughed behind me.

I didn’t stop running until I’d locked myself inside one of the bathroom stalls. Sitting on the toilet, I looked down and saw the erection hadn’t gone down yet. What the hell is going on here?

I had a few minutes of peace before I heard someone calling out for me. I started to panic until I realized it was Becky. “John? Where are you?”

Forgetting for a moment that Becky was in the boys' locker room, I yelled out, “I’m in the bathroom.”

It took a few seconds before Becky found the stall that I was in. Getting to my feet, I opened the door and looked at her. Without saying a word, she embraced me. After a few minutes of this, she said, “Are you okay?”

“No, I’m not okay!” looking at Becky, I said, “I like girls! I shouldn’t be getting turned on by guys, let alone one like Collin.”

Trying to sound reassuring, Becky said, “I’m sure it was just a one-time thing.” Hugging me again, she said, “Give it a few days, and I’m sure everyone will forget it ever happened.”
121.title: Embarrassment in Gym

1211.content:
I should have placed more faith in Becky’s prophecy: by Wednesday, everyone seemed to have forgotten about what happened in the gym. Everyone except Collin; he was still avoiding me. What’s weird is how I felt the few times I met him in the halls: my heart would start racing and I’d have trouble breathing.

I pulled Becky aside after school to ask for her opinion. We walked to the park across the street from school, where she listened to everything I had to say. Once I was finished, she said, “You know what I think? I think you’ve got a crush on Collin.”

“What!?” aghast, I said. “The hell I do! We’re both guys.”

“In case you haven’t noticed, guys can like other guys.” Sighing, Becky said, “Now, I’m not saying you’re gay, because I happen to know you’ve got a crush on me. But at the very least you’re bi.”

I was stunned; could Becky be right? Getting up from the bench, I said, “Thanks for the pep-talk, Becky. This may not have gone the way I wanted it, but I appreciate it.”

Grabbing my arm as I started walking, Becky pulled me back and said, “John, whatever happens, know that I’ll be here to support you.” With a grin, Becky said, “I’m not going to let something like you having a crush on my future husband get in the way of our friendship.”

I wasn't thrilled by Becky's joke, but that didn't stop me from playing along. "What do you mean your husband? Girl, you'll have to fight me for him."

As I started walking towards the exit, neither of us realized just how right I was.
1211.title: Confusing Developments

12111.content:
The park was only a 15-minute walk from home; time I took to think about everything that’s happened to me lately. I always knew puberty was going to be interesting, I just didn’t realize it would be this interesting.

Rounding the corner of my street, I saw my parents standing in the front yard talking with three people that I didn’t recognize. Moving closer, I realized they were Asian, and one of them was a girl about my age. Spotting me, Mom said, “John, why don’t you come say hello to the Hosokawa family. They’re moving in across the street.” Gesturing to the girl, she said, “This is their daughter, Keiko.”

After Introducing myself to the new neighbors, I turned my attention to Keiko. She looked nervous about something, but I couldn’t figure out what. “Keiko, sweetie, why don’t you get to know the neighbor boy?” looking at me, her mom said, “I’m sorry John; Keiko is very self-conscious about her English skills. I keep telling her they’re fine, but what do I know?”

It was clear from the look on her face that Keiko was less than pleased with her mom; and, given everything that happened today, I wasn’t really in the mood for company. But we both decided to suck it up as the two of us walked through the side gate into the backyard.

Sitting on the porch swing, I turned to Keiko and said, “So Keiko, why don’t you tell me about yourself?”

“Afraid there’s not much to say; I didn’t even want to move here.” Sighing, Keiko said, “But father got a new job, so I had to leave every friend I’d ever had.”

“Wow, that sucks.” Looking at her, I said, “I can be your friend if you want; your first friend in your new home.”

Keiko considered my offer for a few moments, “Thank you John; I am, how you say, touched by your gift.”

“It wasn’t so much a gift as it was an offer; but you’re welcome.” Smiling, I said, “If you ever feel like you need help with English, my friends and I will be more than willing to help you.”

“Thank you.”
12111.title: A New Friend

15.content:

info.description:
This is the story of John Saunders, who turns into Heather as the result of a rare genetic condition.

Of course, you've gotta have rules:

1) Do not kill off the main character.
2) Try for correct spelling and punctuation.
3) 1st person, past tense.
4) Keep it PG-13. Nudity is okay. Strong language is okay, but don't overdo it. Sex is okay, but don't go into detail.
5) Once a character changes sex, there's no changing back.
6) It's a rare condition, so let's not have too many characters changing sex.
7) Check out my other interactives for an idea of how I roll.
